rulings.Case Title: LEONARD CERVANTES VS. GOODLEAP, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 1, 2024, line 2, PETITIONER LEONARD CERVANTES'S Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award Plaintiff's unopposed Petition to Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award is granted. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR VS. SHRYNE GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Modifying Protective Order Re Dfp5 And Imposing $6,855 Monetary Sanction; Declaration; And, Memorandum.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 1, 2024, line 4, 1 - PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO'S Motion For Order Modifying Protective Order Re Dfp5 And Imposing $6,855 Monetary Sanction The court adopts the Revised Report and Recommendation of JPT Cohen, which states: In his moving papers, Plaintiff seeks a modification of the protective order to allow him to review the photographs of all of Defendant's customers in order to determine their race. This is an expensive proposition not warranted here. Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.060(f)(4) provides that the Court "shall limit the extent of discovery of electronically stored information, even from a source that is reasonably accessible, if the court determines that any of the following conditions exist: . . . (4) The likely burden or expense of the proposed discovery outweighs the likely benefit, taking into account the amount in controversy, the resources of the parties, the importance of the issues in the litigation, and the importance of the requested discovery in resolving the issues." Code Civ. Proc. 2031.060(f)(4) (emphasis added). Vexatious litigant DiGiacomo's only argument in Dept 525 was that there should be a court reporter so that he could preserve issues for appeal. He has no right to a court reporter under the circumstances of this case and his motion should be denied. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: DEBBIE JOSE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 1, 2024, line 17, 2 - DEFENDANTS SHAREEN MCSPADDEN , IN HER INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY AS DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF HOMELESSNESS AND SUPPORTIVE HOUSING, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Shareen McSpadden's motion to strike punitive damages is granted. First, Government Code section 818 bars punitive damages against the City only. (Runyon v. Superior Ct. (1986) 187 Cal. App. 3d 878, 881 ["Section 818 setting forth the immunity from punitive damages expressly pertains only to public entities. There is no corresponding immunity provision pertaining to public employees. Thus, a public employee may be liable for punitive damages like a private person but unlike a public entity which is specifically immune"].) Second, Jose fails to allege ultimate facts of McSpadden's oppression, fraud, or malice. (Civ. Code 3294.) The gravamen of Jose's complaint with respect to McSpadden is that McSpadden negligently hired and retained the non-profit WeHope and failed to supervise WeHope when it hired the assailant. (Compl. para. 61, 69.) These allegations of negligence do not support the inference that McSpadden hired WeHope with malice towards the shelter residents, or to oppress or commit fraud upon them. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. ELLA + MILA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 1, 2024, line 12, PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment The unopposed Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and Consent Judgment is granted. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: DANILO P QUIAZON VS. RICO QUIAZON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 1, 2024, line 14, DEFENDANT RICO QUIAZON DEMURRER to COMPLAINT *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Defendant Rico Quiazon's demurrer is overruled in party and sustained without leave to amend in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. (CCP 452(h); TSMC North America v. Semiconductor Manufacturing Int'l Corp. (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 581, 597 n.7; In re Marriage of Taschen (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 681, 688 n.3.) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RCE)
